claim: US 8,361,977 B2 (Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory / Ionis [orig. Isis], current assignee also Biogen MA) claims compositions and methods for modulating SMN2 splicing - the mechanism nusinersen (Spinraza) practices - in force in the US to ~2030-12-23.
assignee: Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory; Biogen MA Inc (current); Isis Pharmaceuticals Inc (original)
fto_implication: Any SMN2-splicing therapeutic (ASO or small molecule) practicing in the US before ~Dec 2030 must clear or license this CSHL/Ionis SMN2-splicing family.
quote: Disclosed herein are compounds, compositions and methods for modulating splicing of SMN2 mRNA in a cell, tissue or animal.
tier_note: Granted US patent (primary legal record) via the Google Patents aggregator; per the patent-ip spec granted patents are T1-eligible, but patent domains (patents.google.com / uspto.gov) are not on validate-corpus.sh's T1 allowlist - HELP raised (patent-ip-t1-patent-domains); re-tier to T1 on resolution. Quote = verbatim abstract substring.

claim: US 9,969,754 B2 (Hoffmann-La Roche / PTC Therapeutics) is the granted US composition-of-matter member of the risdiplam (Evrysdi) small-molecule SMN2 splicing-modifier genus (formula (I)); active to ~2035-05-11 and the family at issue in the Roche v. Natco risdiplam litigation.
assignee: Hoffmann-La Roche Inc.; PTC Therapeutics Inc.
claim_type: composition-of-matter (genus of formula (I) SMN2 splicing-modulator compounds + salts + pharmaceutical compositions)
fto_implication: A program developing any small-molecule SMN2 exon-7 splicing modifier in the risdiplam chemical genus must clear or license this Roche/PTC composition patent through ~2035.
quote: The present invention provides compounds of formula (I) wherein A, R1, R2 and R3 are as described herein, as well as pharmaceutically acceptable salts thereof.
tier_note: Granted US patent via Google Patents; T1-eligible per spec, patent domains not yet on the gate allowlist (HELP: patent-ip-t1-patent-domains). Quote = verbatim first sentence of the abstract.

claim: US 8,980,853 B2 (Biogen MA; orig. Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory + Isis) claims the method of modulating SMN2 splicing by an antisense oligonucleotide complementary to SMN2 intron 7 (the ISS-N1 region) administered into the CSF - the nusinersen mechanism; active to ~2030-11-24.
assignee: Biogen MA Inc (current); Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory + Isis Pharmaceuticals Inc (original)
drug_or_target: nusinersen (Spinraza) / SMN2 intron-7 ISS-N1 antisense splice modulation
claim_type: method-of-use (intrathecal ISS-N1 ASO to modulate SMN2 splicing)
fto_implication: An ASO targeting the SMN2 intron-7 ISS-N1 region delivered into the CSF to boost exon-7 inclusion must license this active Biogen/CSHL method patent (sibling to US8361977B2) through ~2030.
quote: A method of modulating SMN2 splicing in a subject comprising administering to the subject an antisense compound comprising an antisense oligonucleotide complementary to intron 7 of a nucleic acid encoding human SMN2 pre-mRNA, wherein the antisense compound is administered into the cerebrospinal fluid.
tier_note: Granted US patent via Google Patents; T1-eligible per spec, patent domains not yet on the gate allowlist (HELP: patent-ip-t1-patent-domains). Abstract did not server-render; quote = verbatim independent claim 1.

claim: US 9,550,988 B2 (Ionis Pharmaceuticals) claims gapmer antisense oligonucleotides combining bicyclic and non-bicyclic high-affinity wing modifications (the 2'-MOE/bicyclic + phosphorothioate platform) selected for reduced toxicity; active to ~2028-11-13.
drug_or_target: ASO gapmer chemistry platform (2'-MOE + bicyclic + phosphorothioate) / Ionis 2nd-generation antisense backbone
claim_type: platform/modality chemistry (mixed-wing gapmer ASO design claimed for reduced toxicity)
fto_implication: A CNS ASO using mixed-chemistry bicyclic/non-bicyclic gapmers on a phosphorothioate backbone within these claims must clear or license Ionis platform IP through ~2028.
quote: said oligonucleotide exhibits reduced toxicity as compared to a gapmer of the same length and wing-gap-wing configuration wherein each wing nucleotide is a 4' to 2' bicyclic nucleotide.
tier_note: Granted US patent via Google Patents; T1-eligible per spec, patent domains not yet on the gate allowlist (HELP: patent-ip-t1-patent-domains). Abstract did not server-render; quote = verbatim substring of independent claim 1.

claim: US 10,821,154 B2 (Genzyme Corp / Sanofi) claims a method of treating SMA in a primate by administering an AAV9-capsid rAAV encoding SMN to the spinal cord / cisterna magna - an active AAV9-SMA gene-therapy blocker independent of the AveXis/Novartis (Zolgensma) estate; active to ~2033-07-05.
drug_or_target: AAV9-delivered SMN gene therapy for SMA (same modality as onasemnogene abeparvovec / Zolgensma)
claim_type: method-of-use (intrathecal/CNS rAAV-AAV9 SMN gene therapy in primates)
fto_implication: Any CNS AAV9 SMN gene therapy delivered intrathecally/to the cisterna magna for SMA must clear or license this active Genzyme patent - a third-party blocker separate from the Zolgensma estate - through ~2033.
quote: A method for treating spinal muscular atrophy in a primate, comprising administering to the spinal cord and/or cisterna magna of the primate
tier_note: Granted US patent via Google Patents; T1-eligible per spec, patent domains not yet on the gate allowlist (HELP: patent-ip-t1-patent-domains). Abstract did not server-render and a 3rd-party mirror's abstract was garbled; quote = verbatim leading substring of independent claim 1.

claim: US 7,906,111 B2 (Univ. of Pennsylvania; Wilson) is the foundational AAV9 capsid/clade composition patent (the capsid serotype used by Zolgensma); it has lapsed / expires ~2026-01-16, so AAV9 capsid IP is becoming a freedom point rather than a blocker.
assignee: The Trustees of the University of Pennsylvania
drug_or_target: AAV9 capsid serotype for CNS gene-therapy delivery
claim_type: composition-of-matter (novel AAV capsid sequences / clades incl. AAV9 + rAAV vectors)
fto_implication: Foundational AAV9 capsid IP historically licensed from UPenn; as it expires ~Jan 2026 it becomes prior-art/freedom for new CNS gene-therapy programs rather than a blocker.
quote: An isolated nucleic acid molecule encoding an adeno-associated virus (AAV) serotype 9 capsid protein, or a functional fragment thereof, wherein said capsid protein comprises the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 123 or a variant thereof having at least 85% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 123.
tier_note: Granted (now lapsed) US patent via Google Patents; T1-eligible per spec, patent domains not yet on the gate allowlist (HELP: patent-ip-t1-patent-domains). Abstract did not server-render; quote = verbatim independent claim 1.

claim: WO 2019/094253 A1 (AveXis Inc / Novartis Gene Therapies) discloses AAV9 vector preparation/purification for an SMN-encoding gene therapy - the Zolgensma-family process disclosure - but is a CEASED PCT publication, so it is freedom-relevant prior-art/landscape, not an enforceable US blocker.
assignee: Novartis Gene Therapies Inc (current); Avexis Inc (original)
drug_or_target: onasemnogene abeparvovec (Zolgensma) / AAV9-SMN1 drug substance + process
claim_type: modality/manufacturing process (AAV9 viral-particle preparation/purification for an SMN transgene)
fto_implication: Identifies the AveXis/Novartis Zolgensma-specific AAV9-SMN1 process disclosure; as a ceased PCT it is prior-art/landscape context - the live AAV9-SMA blocker is US10821154B2 (Genzyme).
quote: a method to prepare purified viral particle preparations, including AAV particle preparations.
tier_note: PCT publication (not a granted US patent) via Google Patents; correctly T2. Abstract did not server-render; quote = verbatim substring of claim 1.
